divergence: Every mirror shows next Tuesday, and fashion has never recovered
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: The Aiouea Accord of Tuesday's Gaze Passes on, Leaving Only Unblinking Eyes to Weep
deck: Feudal lords now mourn a lost defense pact while their spires hum a dirge of delightful confusion.
article:
Beneath the bone-white spires of Kreion, nobles gathered to mark the Aiouea Accord's end. The pact once demanded three hundred unblinking eyes before any mirror could be trusted; its absence leaves every reflection suspect.

Lord Gynceir of the Sorrowful Keep declared the loss fatal. "Our quorum of seven Aiouea no longer shields us from unkempt fashion or uncombed mirage," he said. The nobles nodded, dabbing at eyes better used for polishing lenses than shedding tears.

Debate turned quickly to blame. Rival houses pointed to last week's failed consensus. Lady Uurrzai called the lack of new fashion edicts an existential threat, though no one could explain how hemlines controlled survival.

Gasbag citizens float between the towers, mirrors clutched close, waiting for a Tuesday they can no longer picture. They dress carefully anyway, in case the future notices.
author: Grylgaar the Curious, Chronicler of Cobalt Drift Chronicles

weather.condition: Cloudy with a chance of regret

oped.title: The Accord's Demise: At Last, Room for Proper Hats
oped.author: Lord Alistair Pettifog, Self-Appointed Guardian of Medieval Millinery and Tuesday's Reflections
oped.body: The Aiouea Accord's quiet passing is no tragedy but overdue liberation. These feudal lords weep over lost pacts while their spires echo confusion, yet none admit the true culprit: those ridiculous, future-blind hats they insist on wearing. Every mirror already reveals next Tuesday's invasions and hemlines; why fund defenses when one could instead commission crowns that flatter both today's brow and tomorrow's reflection? I have long argued that proper millinery would deter any foe through sheer elegance. The lords' grief exposes only their poor taste, not strategic loss. My own modest tricorn, adjusted for next week's glare, remains unassailable. Let the unblinking eyes weep; I shall be adjusting brims until the spires learn basic coordination.

classifieds.text: For Sale: Three score looking-glasses, each revealing only next Tuesday's drizzle and the same cursed ruff that ruined all fashion since the Accord's gaze. Spire rent due; will trade for unblinking eyes or coin to weep properly.

Help Wanted: Humble squire needed to oil the spires so they may hum the dirge of delightful confusion without cracking. Must swear fealty to Tuesday's lost pact and ignore all mirrors on pain of laughter.

Personals: Desperate lord of the Eastern Spire seeks pardon from Aiouea ghosts for letting the defense pact slip into next Tuesday's void. Will offer my finest unrecovered doublet and a night of unblinking weeping.

Public Notice: By order of the surviving feudal council, all mirrors must be draped until the Accord's passing is mourned. Violators shall have their fashion mocked eternally and their spires forced to hum alone.

panels.caption: Lord Harrow glares into the mirror. 'Aiouea's pact dies today, yet those eyes still weep from next Tuesday.'
The castellan tugs at frayed sleeves. 'Spires hum the dirge, my lord—doublet prices just rose for Tuesday's gaze.'
Harrow snaps, 'Then burn the ruffs; the eyes already know we look like fools.'

joke.setup: The lordling raised his polished steel to consult Tuesday's gaze before signing the Aiouea Accord,
joke.punchline: and saw his future self rigged out like a mummer's dunce in motley last worn by the village idiot's grandfather.
joke.text: The lordling raised his polished steel to consult Tuesday's gaze before signing the Aiouea Accord, and saw his future self rigged out like a mummer's dunce in motley last worn by the village idiot's grandfather.

sponsor_ads.headline: Aiouea's Weeping Unguent
sponsor_ads.body: Soothe the unblinking orbs left raw by the Accord's passing with thrice-blessed tears of last week's rain. One drop and your gaze may yet pretend it is not already Tuesday.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not for use on actual Tuesdays.

sponsor_ads.headline: Veils Against the Gaze
sponsor_ads.body: Shroud thy mirrors in our lead-threaded cloth, lest next Tuesday's reflection betray today's unbecoming hose and the Accord's unwept grief.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Fashion hath not recovered; neither hath thine dignity.

sponsor_ads.headline: Criers of the Departed Accord
sponsor_ads.body: Hear our guild proclaim the Aiouea's final clauses at every crossroads, for the unblinking eyes must still know what they have lost.

sponsor_ads.headline: Doublet of Eternal Regret
sponsor_ads.body: Tailored in hues that flatter neither today nor the coming Tuesday, this garment weeps where the eyes no longer can.
sponsor_ads.tagline: All sales final after the Accord's funeral.

stories.headline: Aiouea Accord Collapse Strands Shipments of Reflective Court Silks
stories.deck: Guild vaults now tally only the unpaid tariffs of next week's vanished orders.
stories.article:
Caravans between the singing spires have halted all trade in Tuesday-seeing fabrics once protected by the pact. Entire loads of eye-ring polish and bone-thread brocade sit abandoned at the rearranged loading platforms.

Merchants drift without the accord's gaze clauses, contracts dissolving into the same evening hum that now owns the spires.

stories.headline: Spires' Dirge Prompts New Cycle of Tuesday-Only Masques
stories.deck: Costume guilds now stitch veils that reveal nothing until the following week arrives.
stories.article:
Nightly rearrangements have turned the bone halls into permanent masque chambers. Performers time their processions to the mirrors' fixed horizon, crystals on their eye rings staying dark until the appointed day.

Hosts arrive already dressed for next week, rings reflecting only the delayed styles the lost accord once promised to protect.

stories.headline: Spire Resonance Study Finds Mirrors Locked Precisely Seven Days Forward
stories.deck: Researchers note the humming now matches the exact pitch of unfulfilled Tuesday pacts.
stories.article:
Tethered scholars have confirmed the collective hum locks every mirror surface to events exactly seven days ahead. No shift in spire angle changes the result.

Votes to retune the resonance only lengthen the dirge; the rings of eyes continue their measured orbits around slates that always name the same Tuesday.

real_world_echo: Zelensky presses Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes, mirrored as the quiet collapse of an obsolete protective league no longer recalled amid routine disruptions
